title: trachyspermum stewartii
intro: Trachyspermum stewartii, also known as ajwain, is a plant species that belongs to the parsley family (Apiaceae) and is native to India and other parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Trachyspermum stewartii include its carminative, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the essential oils present in its seeds. The health benefits of consuming Trachyspermum stewartii include relief from digestive issues, such as bloating, gas, and indigestion, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Trachyspermum stewartii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
